document_id: 3.5 NC ESG Third Party Verification of Client's Income 2021.v1.pdf

findings.description: The 'Please return this form to' section in the PDF has a two-column layout (Name & Title / Phone, Address / Fax, Email) which is collapsed to a single-column layout in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Name & Title with Phone on the same line, Address with Fax on the same line. HTML shows Name & Title with Phone below it, Address with Fax below it, losing the side-by-side layout.

findings.description: The CHECK ONE section layout changed from a multi-column grid layout in the PDF to a single-column bullet list in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows checkboxes arranged in three columns (e.g., Social Security/SSI | Pension/Retirement | TANF on one row). HTML shows them as a single-column bulleted list.

findings.description: The Employment Income section and Payments and/or Benefit Income section are enclosed in visible bordered boxes in the PDF but appear without such visual boxing in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF has checkbox sections visually separated by bordered rectangles. HTML lacks these visual containers.

findings.description: The employer section text about pay has slightly different line breaking, with 'He/she is paid' appearing at end of line in PDF but wrapping differently in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: '...since ___. He/she is paid $___' on separate line. HTML: '...since ___. He/she is paid $___' reformatted into different line breaks.

findings.description: The 'Payments and/or Benefit Income' heading text is rendered at a larger font size in HTML compared to the PDF, making it more prominent than in the original.
findings.evidence: PDF shows this as a normal-sized checkbox label. HTML renders it as a large heading-style text spanning multiple lines.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements, which is a structural duplication of the main wrapper element, though the content itself is not duplicated.
findings.evidence: HTML has <main> inside <main>, but no actual content is repeated.

findings.description: The section labels 'Employment Income' and 'Payments and/or Benefit Income' are rendered as h2 headings in the HTML, but in the PDF they are checkbox-labeled section dividers, not hierarchical headings. More importantly, the checkbox options under 'CHECK ONE' in the PDF are presented in a grid/table-like layout (3 columns), but in the HTML they are rendered as a simple bulleted list, which changes the visual structure but not the content.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Employment Income' and 'Payments and/or Benefit Income' as checkbox-prefixed section labels with horizontal rule separators. HTML renders them as '☐ Employment Income' and '☐ Payments and/or Benefit Income' as h2 headings.
